title: Intensely Garlicky French Lentils Tarte Tatin
description: A cozy French dish featuring lentils and garlic, perfect for a flexitarian dinner, this tarte tatin is a twist on the classic recipe, with a focus on intense garlic flavor and bouncy lentils.

ingredients.name: lentils
ingredients.notes: green or brown lentils work well

instructions: Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large saucepan, saute the chopped onion and minced garlic in olive oil until the onion is translucent.
Add the chopped carrots, lentils, tomato paste, thyme, salt, and pepper to the saucepan, and stir to combine.
Add enough water to the saucepan to cover the lentils, and bring to a boil.
Reduce the heat to low, cover the saucepan, and simmer the lentil mixture for 30 minutes, or until the lentils are tender.
On a lightly floured surface, roll out the puff pastry to a large square, about 1/4 inch thick.
Spoon the lentil mixture into the center of the pastry square, leaving a 1 inch border around the edges.
Fold the edges of the pastry up over the filling, pressing gently to seal.
Place the tarte tatin on a baking sheet, and bake for 25 minutes, or until the pastry is golden brown.
Remove the tarte tatin from the oven, and let it cool for 10 minutes before serving.
